content:
{
  "title": "Bias-Contrastive Prompting: Dynamic Counterfactual Prompts to Mitigate Social Stereotypes in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often perpetuate social biases and stereotypes in their outputs, even when prompts are phrased neutrally. Existing prompting methods inadequately address these biases at inference time.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed in sensitive applications, mitigating social biases is critical to ensure fair and equitable language generation. Recent advances in prompt engineering focus on accuracy or controllability, but methods explicitly targeting bias reduction remain sparse. Addressing this gap is crucial as models are increasingly used in high-stakes, real-world settings.",
  "method": "We propose Bias-Contrastive Prompting (BCP), a novel prompting technique that augments user prompts with dynamic, counterfactual sub-prompts representing protected attributes (e.g., gender, race) with alternative values. For each input, BCP generates parallel completions for multiple attribute instantiations, then computes the symmetric difference across outputs to identify and suppress biased continuations during decoding, using a gating mechanism. This method is model-agnostic and requires no further fine-tuning, operating entirely at inference time.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate BCP on datasets designed to reveal social biases (e.g., StereoSet, Winogender) and compare against standard prompts, debiasing prompts, and post-hoc re-ranking baselines. Metrics will include bias scores (e.g., StereoSet intrasentence and intersentence), human preference ratings, and utility preservation. Ablation studies will assess the impact of different counterfactual prompt generation strategies."
}
